NKJV.1: Then Jesus, being filled with the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness,
NKJV.2: being tempted for forty days by the devil. And in those days He ate nothing, and afterward, when they had ended, He was hungry.
NKJV.3: And the devil said to Him, “If You are the Son of God, command this stone to become bread.”
NKJV.4: But Jesus answered him, saying, “It is written,  ‘Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.’ ”
NKJV.5: Then the devil, taking Him up on a high mountain, showed Him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.
NKJV.6: And the devil said to Him, “All this authority I will give You, and their glory; for this has been delivered to me, and I give it to whomever I wish.
NKJV.7: Therefore, if You will worship before me, all will be Yours.”
NKJV.8: And Jesus answered and said to him, “Get behind Me, Satan! For it is written, ‘You shall worship the LORD your God, and Him only you shall serve.’ ”
NKJV.9: Then he brought Him to Jerusalem, set Him on the pinnacle of the temple, and said to Him, “If You are the Son of God, throw Yourself down from here.
NKJV.10: For it is written:  ‘He shall give His angels charge over you, To keep you,’
NKJV.11: and, ‘In their hands they shall bear you up, Lest you dash your foot against a stone.’ ”
NKJV.12: And Jesus answered and said to him, “It has been said,  ‘You shall not tempt the LORD your God.’ ”
NKJV.13: Now when the devil had ended every temptation, he departed from Him until an opportune time.
NKJV.14: Then Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and news of Him went out through all the surrounding region.
NKJV.15: And He taught in their synagogues, being glorified by all.
NKJV.16: So He came to Nazareth, where He had been brought up. And as His custom was, He went into the synagogue on the Sabbath day, and stood up to read.
NKJV.17: And He was handed the book of the prophet Isaiah. And when He had opened the book, He found the place where it was written:
NKJV.18: “The Spirit of the LORD is upon Me, Because He has anointed Me To preach the gospel to the poor; He has sent Me to heal the brokenhearted, To proclaim liberty to the captives And recovery of sight to the blind, To set at liberty those who are oppressed;
NKJV.19: To proclaim the acceptable year of the LORD.”
NKJV.20: Then He closed the book, and gave it back to the attendant and sat down. And the eyes of all who were in the synagogue were fixed on Him.
NKJV.21: And He began to say to them, “Today this Scripture is  fulfilled in your hearing.”
NKJV.22: So all bore witness to Him, and marveled at the gracious words which proceeded out of His mouth. And they said, “Is this not Joseph’s son?”
NKJV.23: He said to them, “You will surely say this proverb to Me, ‘Physician, heal yourself! Whatever we have heard done in  Capernaum, do also here in  Your country.’ ”
NKJV.24: Then He said, “Assuredly, I say to you, no  prophet is accepted in his own country.
NKJV.25: But I tell you truly,  many widows were in Israel in the days of Elijah, when the heaven was shut up three years and six months, and there was a great famine throughout all the land;
NKJV.26: but to none of them was Elijah sent except to Zarephath, in the region of Sidon, to a woman who was a widow.
NKJV.27: And many lepers were in Israel in the time of Elisha the prophet, and none of them was cleansed except Naaman the Syrian.”
NKJV.28: So all those in the synagogue, when they heard these things, were filled with wrath,
NKJV.29: and rose up and thrust Him out of the city; and they led Him to the brow of the hill on which their city was built, that they might throw Him down over the cliff.
NKJV.30: Then passing through the midst of them, He went His way.
NKJV.31: Then He went down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and was teaching them on the Sabbaths.
NKJV.32: And they were astonished at His teaching, for His word was with authority.
NKJV.33: Now in the synagogue there was a man who had a spirit of an unclean demon. And he cried out with a loud voice,
NKJV.34: saying, “Let us alone! What have we to do with You, Jesus of Nazareth? Did You come to destroy us? I know who You are— the Holy One of God!”
NKJV.35: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be quiet, and come out of him!” And when the demon had thrown him in their midst, it came out of him and did not hurt him.
NKJV.36: Then they were all amazed and spoke among themselves, saying, “What a word this is! For with authority and power He commands the unclean spirits, and they come out.”
NKJV.37: And the report about Him went out into every place in the surrounding region.
NKJV.38: Now He arose from the synagogue and entered Simon’s house. But Simon’s wife’s mother was sick with a high fever, and they made request of Him concerning her.
NKJV.39: So He stood over her and rebuked the fever, and it left her. And immediately she arose and served them.
NKJV.40: When the sun was setting, all those who had any that were sick with various diseases brought them to Him; and He laid His hands on every one of them and healed them.
NKJV.41: And demons also came out of many, crying out and saying, “You are the Christ, the Son of God!” And He, rebuking them, did not allow them to speak, for they knew that He was the Christ.
NKJV.42: Now when it was day, He departed and went into a deserted place. And the crowd sought Him and came to Him, and tried to keep Him from leaving them;
NKJV.43: but He said to them, “I must  preach the kingdom of God to the other cities also, because for this purpose I have been sent.”
NKJV.44: And He was preaching in the synagogues of Galilee.

ESV.1: And Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan and was led by the Spirit in the wilderness
ESV.2: for forty days, being tempted by the devil. And he ate nothing during those days. And when they were ended, he was hungry.
ESV.3: The devil said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command this stone to become bread.”
ESV.4: And Jesus answered him,  “It is written, ‘Man shall not live by bread alone.’”
ESV.5: And the devil took him up and showed him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time,
ESV.6: and said to him, “To you I will give all this authority and their glory, for it has been delivered to me, and I give it to whom I will.
ESV.7: If you, then, will worship me, it will all be yours.”
ESV.8: And Jesus answered him,  “It is written,  “‘You shall worship the Lord your God, and him only shall you serve.’”
ESV.9: And he took him to Jerusalem and set him on the pinnacle of the temple and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down from here,
ESV.10: for it is written, “‘He will command his angels concerning you, to guard you,’
ESV.11: and “‘On their hands they will bear you up, lest you strike your foot against a stone.’”
ESV.12: And Jesus answered him, “It is said, ‘You shall not put the Lord your God to the test.’”
ESV.13: And when the devil had ended every temptation, he departed from him until an opportune time.
ESV.14: And Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and a report about him went out through all the surrounding country.
ESV.15: And he taught in their synagogues, being glorified by all.
ESV.16: And he came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up. And as was his custom, he went to the synagogue on the Sabbath day, and he stood up to read.
ESV.17: And the scroll of the prophet Isaiah was given to him. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written,
ESV.18: “The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor.  He has sent me to proclaim liberty to the captives and recovering of sight to the blind,  to set at liberty those who are oppressed,
ESV.19: to proclaim the year of the Lord's favor.”
ESV.20: And he rolled up the scroll and gave it back to the attendant and sat down. And the eyes of all in the synagogue were fixed on him.
ESV.21: And he began to say to them, “Today this Scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing.”
ESV.22: And all spoke well of him and marveled at the gracious words that were coming from his mouth. And they said, “Is not this Joseph's son?”
ESV.23: And he said to them, “Doubtless you will quote to me this proverb, ‘“Physician, heal yourself.” What we have heard you did at Capernaum, do here in your hometown as well.’”
ESV.24: And he said, “Truly, I say to you, no prophet is acceptable in his hometown.
ESV.25: But in truth, I tell you, there were many widows in Israel in the days of Elijah, when the heavens were shut up three years and six months, and a great famine came over all the land,
ESV.26: and Elijah was sent to none of them but only to Zarephath, in the land of Sidon, to a woman who was a widow.
ESV.27: And there were many lepers  in Israel in the time of the prophet Elisha, and none of them was cleansed, but only Naaman the Syrian.”
ESV.28: When they heard these things, all in the synagogue were filled with wrath.
ESV.29: And they rose up and drove him out of the town and brought him to the brow of the hill on which their town was built, so that they could throw him down the cliff.
ESV.30: But passing through their midst, he went away.
ESV.31: And he went down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee. And he was teaching them on the Sabbath,
ESV.32: and they were astonished at his teaching, for his word possessed authority.
ESV.33: And in the synagogue there was a man who had the spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried out with a loud voice,
ESV.34: “Ha!  What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are — the Holy One of God.”
ESV.35: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be silent and come out of him!” And when the demon had thrown him down in their midst, he came out of him, having done him no harm.
ESV.36: And they were all amazed and said to one another, “What is this word? For with authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and they come out!”
ESV.37: And reports about him went out into every place in the surrounding region.
ESV.38: And he arose and left the synagogue and entered Simon's house. Now Simon's mother-in-law was ill with a high fever, and they appealed to him on her behalf.
ESV.39: And he stood over her and rebuked the fever, and it left her, and immediately she rose and began to serve them.
ESV.40: Now when the sun was setting, all those who had any who were sick with various diseases brought them to him, and he laid his hands on every one of them and healed them.
ESV.41: And demons also came out of many, crying, “You are the Son of God!” But he rebuked them and would not allow them to speak, because they knew that he was the Christ.
ESV.42: And when it was day, he departed and went into a desolate place. And the people sought him and came to him, and would have kept him from leaving them,
ESV.43: but he said to them,  “I must preach the good news of the kingdom of God to the other towns as well; for I was sent for this purpose.”
ESV.44: And he was preaching in the synagogues of Judea.

NASB.1: Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan and was led around by the Spirit in the wilderness
NASB.2: for forty days, being tempted by the devil. And He ate nothing during those days, and when they had ended, He became hungry.
NASB.3: And the devil said to Him, 'If You are the Son of God, tell this stone to become bread.'
NASB.4: And Jesus answered him, 'It is written, 'MAN SHALL NOT LIVE ON BREAD ALONE.''
NASB.5: And he led Him up and showed Him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.
NASB.6: And the devil said to Him, 'I will give You all this domain and its glory; for it has been handed over to me, and I give it to whomever I wish.
NASB.7: 'Therefore if You worship before me, it shall all be Yours.'
NASB.8: Jesus answered him, 'It is written, 'YOU SHALL WORSHIP THE LORD YOUR GOD AND SERVE HIM ONLY.''
NASB.9: And he led Him to Jerusalem and had Him stand on the pinnacle of the temple, and said to Him, 'If You are the Son of God, throw Yourself down from here;
NASB.10: for it is written, 'HE WILL COMMAND HIS ANGELS CONCERNING YOU TO GUARD YOU,'
NASB.11: and, 'ON [their] HANDS THEY WILL BEAR YOU UP, SO THAT YOU WILL NOT STRIKE YOUR FOOT AGAINST A STONE.''
NASB.12: And Jesus answered and said to him, 'It is said, 'YOU SHALL NOT PUT THE LORD YOUR GOD TO THE TEST.''
NASB.13: When the devil had finished every temptation, he left Him until an opportune time.
NASB.14: And Jesus returned to Galilee in the power of the Spirit, and news about Him spread through all the surrounding district.
NASB.15: And He [began] teaching in their synagogues and was praised by all.
NASB.16: And He came to Nazareth, where He had been brought up; and as was His custom, He entered the synagogue on the Sabbath, and stood up to read.
NASB.17: And the book of the prophet Isaiah was handed to Him. And He opened the book and found the place where it was written,
NASB.18: 'THE SPIRIT OF THE LORD IS UPON ME, BECAUSE HE ANOINTED ME TO PREACH THE GOSPEL TO THE POOR. HE HAS SENT ME TO PROCLAIM RELEASE TO THE CAPTIVES, AND RECOVERY OF SIGHT TO THE BLIND, TO SET FREE THOSE WHO ARE OPPRESSED,
NASB.19: TO PROCLAIM THE FAVORABLE YEAR OF THE LORD.'
NASB.20: And He closed the book, gave it back to the attendant and sat down; and the eyes of all in the synagogue were fixed on Him.
NASB.21: And He began to say to them, 'Today this Scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing.'
NASB.22: And all were speaking well of Him, and wondering at the gracious words which were falling from His lips; and they were saying, 'Is this not Joseph's son?'
NASB.23: And He said to them, 'No doubt you will quote this proverb to Me, 'Physician, heal yourself! Whatever we heard was done at Capernaum, do here in your hometown as well.''
NASB.24: And He said, 'Truly I say to you, no prophet is welcome in his hometown.
NASB.25: 'But I say to you in truth, there were many widows in Israel in the days of Elijah, when the sky was shut up for three years and six months, when a great famine came over all the land;
NASB.26: and yet Elijah was sent to none of them, but only to Zarephath, [in the land] of Sidon, to a woman who was a widow.
NASB.27: 'And there were many lepers in Israel in the time of Elisha the prophet; and none of them was cleansed, but only Naaman the Syrian.'
NASB.28: And all [the people] in the synagogue were filled with rage as they heard these things;
NASB.29: and they got up and drove Him out of the city, and led Him to the brow of the hill on which their city had been built, in order to throw Him down the cliff.
NASB.30: But passing through their midst, He went His way.
NASB.31: And He came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and He was teaching them on the Sabbath;
NASB.32: and they were amazed at His teaching, for His message was with authority.
NASB.33: In the synagogue there was a man possessed by the spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried out with a loud voice,
NASB.34: 'Let us alone! What business do we have with each other, Jesus of Nazareth? Have You come to destroy us? I know who You are-- the Holy One of God!'
NASB.35: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, 'Be quiet and come out of him!' And when the demon had thrown him down in the midst [of the people], he came out of him without doing him any harm.
NASB.36: And amazement came upon them all, and they [began] talking with one another saying, 'What is this message? For with authority and power He commands the unclean spirits and they come out.'
NASB.37: And the report about Him was spreading into every locality in the surrounding district.
NASB.38: Then He got up and [left] the synagogue, and entered Simon's home. Now Simon's mother-in-law was suffering from a high fever, and they asked Him to help her.
NASB.39: And standing over her, He rebuked the fever, and it left her; and she immediately got up and waited on them.
NASB.40: While the sun was setting, all those who had any [who were] sick with various diseases brought them to Him; and laying His hands on each one of them, He was healing them.
NASB.41: Demons also were coming out of many, shouting, 'You are the Son of God!' But rebuking them, He would not allow them to speak, because they knew Him to be the Christ.
NASB.42: When day came, Jesus left and went to a secluded place; and the crowds were searching for Him, and came to Him and tried to keep Him from going away from them.
NASB.43: But He said to them, 'I must preach the kingdom of God to the other cities also, for I was sent for this purpose.'
NASB.44: So He kept on preaching in the synagogues of Judea.

NLT.1: Then Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan River. He was led by the Spirit in the wilderness,
NLT.2: where he was tempted by the devil for forty days. Jesus ate nothing all that time and became very hungry.
NLT.3: Then the devil said to him, “If you are the Son of God, tell this stone to become a loaf of bread.”
NLT.4: But Jesus told him, “No! The Scriptures say, ‘People do not live by bread alone.’ ”
NLT.5: Then the devil took him up and revealed to him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.
NLT.6: “I will give you the glory of these kingdoms and authority over them,” the devil said, “because they are mine to give to anyone I please.
NLT.7: I will give it all to you if you will worship me.”
NLT.8:
Jesus replied, “The Scriptures say, ‘You must worship the LORD your God
and serve only him.’ ”
NLT.9: Then the devil took him to Jerusalem, to the highest point of the Temple, and said, “If you are the Son of God, jump off!
NLT.10: For the Scriptures say, ‘He will order his angels to protect and guard you.
NLT.11:
And they will hold you up with their hands
so you won’t even hurt your foot on a stone.’ ”
NLT.12: Jesus responded, “The Scriptures also say, ‘You must not test the LORD your God.’ ”
NLT.13: When the devil had finished tempting Jesus, he left him until the next opportunity came.
NLT.14: Then Jesus returned to Galilee, filled with the Holy Spirit’s power. Reports about him spread quickly through the whole region.
NLT.15: He taught regularly in their synagogues and was praised by everyone.
NLT.16: When he came to the village of Nazareth, his boyhood home, he went as usual to the synagogue on the Sabbath and stood up to read the Scriptures.
NLT.17: The scroll of Isaiah the prophet was handed to him. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where this was written:
NLT.18:
“The Spirit of the LORD is upon me,
for he has anointed me to bring Good News to the poor.
He has sent me to proclaim that captives will be released,
that the blind will see,
that the oppressed will be set free,
NLT.19: and that the time of the LORD’s favor has come. ”
NLT.20: He rolled up the scroll, handed it back to the attendant, and sat down. All eyes in the synagogue looked at him intently.
NLT.21: Then he began to speak to them. “The Scripture you’ve just heard has been fulfilled this very day!”
NLT.22: Everyone spoke well of him and was amazed by the gracious words that came from his lips. “How can this be?” they asked. “Isn’t this Joseph’s son?”
NLT.23: Then he said, “You will undoubtedly quote me this proverb: ‘Physician, heal yourself’ — meaning, ‘Do miracles here in your hometown like those you did in Capernaum.’
NLT.24: But I tell you the truth, no prophet is accepted in his own hometown.
NLT.25: “Certainly there were many needy widows in Israel in Elijah’s time, when the heavens were closed for three and a half years, and a severe famine devastated the land.
NLT.26: Yet Elijah was not sent to any of them. He was sent instead to a foreigner — a widow of Zarephath in the land of Sidon.
NLT.27: And many in Israel had leprosy in the time of the prophet Elisha, but the only one healed was Naaman, a Syrian.”
NLT.28: When they heard this, the people in the synagogue were furious.
NLT.29: Jumping up, they mobbed him and forced him to the edge of the hill on which the town was built. They intended to push him over the cliff,
NLT.30: but he passed right through the crowd and went on his way.
NLT.31: Then Jesus went to Capernaum, a town in Galilee, and taught there in the synagogue every Sabbath day.
NLT.32: There, too, the people were amazed at his teaching, for he spoke with authority.
NLT.33: Once when he was in the synagogue, a man possessed by a demon — an evil spirit — cried out, shouting,
NLT.34: “Go away! Why are you interfering with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are — the Holy One of God!”
NLT.35: But Jesus reprimanded him. “Be quiet! Come out of the man,” he ordered. At that, the demon threw the man to the floor as the crowd watched; then it came out of him without hurting him further.
NLT.36: Amazed, the people exclaimed, “What authority and power this man’s words possess! Even evil spirits obey him, and they flee at his command!”
NLT.37: The news about Jesus spread through every village in the entire region.
NLT.38: After leaving the synagogue that day, Jesus went to Simon’s home, where he found Simon’s mother-in-law very sick with a high fever. “Please heal her,” everyone begged.
NLT.39: Standing at her bedside, he rebuked the fever, and it left her. And she got up at once and prepared a meal for them.
NLT.40: As the sun went down that evening, people throughout the village brought sick family members to Jesus. No matter what their diseases were, the touch of his hand healed every one.
NLT.41: Many were possessed by demons; and the demons came out at his command, shouting, “You are the Son of God!” But because they knew he was the Messiah, he rebuked them and refused to let them speak.
NLT.42: Early the next morning Jesus went out to an isolated place. The crowds searched everywhere for him, and when they finally found him, they begged him not to leave them.
NLT.43: But he replied, “I must preach the Good News of the Kingdom of God in other towns, too, because that is why I was sent.”
NLT.44: So he continued to travel around, preaching in synagogues throughout Judea.

WEB.1: Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness
WEB.2: for forty days, being tempted by the devil. He ate nothing in those days. Afterward, when they were completed, he was hungry.
WEB.3: The devil said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command this stone to become bread.”
WEB.4: Jesus answered him, saying, “It is written, ‘Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.’”
WEB.5: The devil, leading him up on a high mountain, showed him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.
WEB.6: The devil said to him, “I will give you all this authority, and their glory, for it has been delivered to me; and I give it to whomever I want.
WEB.7: If you therefore will worship before me, it will all be yours.”
WEB.8: Jesus answered him, “Get behind me Satan! For it is written, ‘You shall worship the Lord your God, and you shall serve him only.’”
WEB.9: He led him to Jerusalem, and set him on the pinnacle of the temple, and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, cast yourself down from here,
WEB.10: for it is written, ‘He will put his angels in charge of you, to guard you;’
WEB.11: and, ‘On their hands they will bear you up, lest perhaps you dash your foot against a stone.’”
WEB.12: Jesus answering, said to him, “It has been said, ‘You shall not tempt the Lord your God.’”
WEB.13: When the devil had completed every temptation, he departed from him until another time.
WEB.14: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee, and news about him spread through all the surrounding area.
WEB.15: He taught in their synagogues, being glorified by all.
WEB.16: He came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up. He entered, as was his custom, into the synagogue on the Sabbath day, and stood up to read.
WEB.17: The book of the prophet Isaiah was handed to him. He opened the book, and found the place where it was written,
WEB.18: “The Spirit of the Lord is on me,  because he has anointed me to preach good news to the poor.  He has sent me to heal the broken hearted,   to proclaim release to the captives,  recovering of sight to the blind,  to deliver those who are crushed,
WEB.19: and to proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord.”
WEB.20: He closed the book, gave it back to the attendant, and sat down. The eyes of all in the synagogue were fastened on him.
WEB.21: He began to tell them, “Today, this Scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing.”
WEB.22: All testified about him, and wondered at the gracious words which proceeded out of his mouth, and they said, “Isn’t this Joseph’s son?”
WEB.23: He said to them, “Doubtless you will tell me this parable, ‘Physician, heal yourself! Whatever we have heard done at Capernaum, do also here in your hometown.’”
WEB.24: He said, “Most certainly I tell you, no prophet is acceptable in his hometown.
WEB.25: But truly I tell you, there were many widows in Israel in the days of Elijah, when the sky was shut up three years and six months, when a great famine came over all the land.
WEB.26: Elijah was sent to none of them, except to Zarephath, in the land of Sidon, to a woman who was a widow.
WEB.27: There were many lepers in Israel in the time of Elisha the prophet, yet not one of them was cleansed, except Naaman, the Syrian.”
WEB.28: They were all filled with wrath in the synagogue, as they heard these things.
WEB.29: They rose up, threw him out of the city, and led him to the brow of the hill that their city was built on, that they might throw him off the cliff.
WEB.30: But he, passing through the middle of them, went his way.
WEB.31: He came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee. He was teaching them on the Sabbath day,
WEB.32: and they were astonished at his teaching, for his word was with authority.
WEB.33: In the synagogue there was a man who had a spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried out with a loud voice,
WEB.34: saying, “Ah! what have we to do with you, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know you who you are: the Holy One of God!”
WEB.35: Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be silent, and come out of him!”  When the demon had thrown him down in the middle of them, he came out of him, having done him no harm.
WEB.36: Amazement came on all, and they spoke together, one with another, saying, “What is this word? For with authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and they come out!”
WEB.37: News about him went out into every place of the surrounding region.
WEB.38: He rose up from the synagogue, and entered into Simon’s house. Simon’s mother-in-law was afflicted with a great fever, and they begged him for her.
WEB.39: He stood over her, and rebuked the fever; and it left her. Immediately she rose up and served them.
WEB.40: When the sun was setting, all those who had any sick with various diseases brought them to him; and he laid his hands on every one of them, and healed them.
WEB.41: Demons also came out of many, crying out, and saying, “You are the Christ, the Son of God!” Rebuking them, he didn’t allow them to speak, because they knew that he was the Christ.
WEB.42: When it was day, he departed and went into an uninhabited place, and the multitudes looked for him, and came to him, and held on to him, so that he wouldn’t go away from them.
WEB.43: But he said to them, “I must preach the good news of God’s Kingdom to the other cities also. For this reason I have been sent.”
WEB.44: He was preaching in the synagogues of Galilee.

CSB17.23: Then he said to them, “No doubt you will quote this proverb <sup>[15]</sup>to me: ‘Doctor, heal yourself. What we’ve heard that took place in Capernaum, <sup>ⓖ</sup>do here in your hometown also.’”
CSB17.24: He also said, “Truly I tell you, no prophet is accepted in his hometown. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.25: But I say to you, there were certainly many widows in Israel in Elijah’s days, <sup>ⓘ</sup> when the sky was shut up for three years and six months while a great famine came over all the land.
CSB17.26: Yet Elijah was not sent to any of them except a widow at Zarephath in Sidon. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.27: And in the prophet Elisha’s time, there were many in Israel who had leprosy, <sup>[16]</sup>and yet not one of them was cleansed except Naaman the Syrian.” <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.28: When they heard this, everyone in the synagogue was enraged.
CSB17.29: They got up, drove him out of town, <sup>ⓛ</sup> and brought him to the edge of the hill that their town was built on, intending to hurl him over the cliff.
CSB17.30: But he passed right through the crowd and went on his way. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.31: Then he went down to Capernaum, <sup>ⓝ</sup> a town in Galilee, and was teaching them on the Sabbath.
CSB17.32: They were astonished at his teaching because his message had authority. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.33: In the synagogue there was a man with an unclean demonic spirit who cried out with a loud voice, <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.34: “Leave us alone! What do you have to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are — the Holy One of God!” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.35: But Jesus rebuked him <sup>ⓓ</sup> and said, “Be silent and come out of him!” <sup>ⓔ</sup> And throwing him down before them, the demon came out of him without hurting him at all.
CSB17.36: Amazement came over them all, and they were saying to one another, “What is this message? For he commands the unclean spirits with authority and power, and they come out!”
CSB17.37: And news about him began to go out to every place in the vicinity.
CSB17.38: After he left the synagogue, he entered Simon’s house. <sup>ⓕ</sup> Simon’s mother-in-law was suffering from a high fever, and they asked him about her. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.39: So he stood over her and rebuked the fever, and it left her. She got up immediately <sup>ⓗ</sup> and began to serve them.
CSB17.40: When the sun was setting, all those who had anyone sick with various diseases brought them to him. As he laid his hands on each one of them, he healed them. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.41: Also, demons were coming out of many, shouting and saying, “You are the Son of God!” <sup>ⓙ</sup> But he rebuked them and would not allow them to speak, because they knew he was the Christ. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.42: When it was day, he went out and made his way to a deserted place. <sup>ⓛ</sup> But the crowds were searching for him. They came to him and tried to keep him from leaving them.
CSB17.43: But he said to them, “It is necessary for me to proclaim the good news about the kingdom of God to the other towns also, because I was sent for this purpose.”
CSB17.44: And he was preaching in the synagogues of Judea. <sup>[17]</sup> <sup>ⓜ</sup>

AMP.1: Now Jesus, full of [and in perfect communication with] the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan and was led by the Spirit in the wilderness
AMP.2: for forty days, being tempted by the devil. And He ate nothing during those days, and when they ended, He was hungry.
AMP.3: Then the devil said to Him, "If You are the Son of God, command this stone to turn into bread."
AMP.4: Jesus replied to him, "It is written and forever remains written, 'Man shall not live by bread alone.'"
AMP.5: Then he led Jesus up [to a high mountain] and displayed before Him all the kingdoms of the inhabited earth [and their magnificence] in the twinkling of an eye.
AMP.6: And the devil said to Him, "I will give You all this realm and its glory [its power, its renown]; because it has been handed over to me, and I give it to whomever I wish.
AMP.7: Therefore if You worship before me, it will all be Yours."
AMP.8: Jesus replied to him, "It is written and forever remains written, 'You shall worship the Lord your God and serve only Him.'"
AMP.9: Then he led Jesus to Jerusalem and had Him stand on the pinnacle (highest point) of the temple, and said [mockingly] to Him, "If You are the Son of God, throw Yourself down from here;
AMP.10:
for it is written and forever remains written,
'He will command His angels concerning You to guard and protect You,'
AMP.11:
and,
'they will lift You up on their hands,
So that You do not strike Your foot against a stone.'"
AMP.12: Jesus replied to him, "It is said [in Scripture], 'you shall not tempt the Lord your God [to prove Himself to you].'"
AMP.13: When the devil had finished every temptation, he [temporarily] left Him until a more opportune time.
AMP.14: Then Jesus went back to Galilee in the power of the Spirit, and the news about Him spread through the entire region.
AMP.15: And He began teaching in their synagogues and was praised and glorified and honored by all.
AMP.16: So He came to Nazareth, where He had been brought up; and as was His custom, He entered the synagogue on the Sabbath, and stood up to read.
AMP.17: The scroll of the prophet Isaiah was handed to Him. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written,
AMP.18:
"The Spirit of the Lord is upon Me (the Messiah),
Because He has anointed Me to preach the good news to the poor.
He has sent Me to announce release (pardon, forgiveness) to the captives,
And recovery of sight to the blind,
To set free those who are oppressed (downtrodden, bruised, crushed by tragedy),
AMP.19: to proclaim the favorable year of the Lord [the day when salvation and the favor of God abound greatly]."
AMP.20: Then He rolled up the scroll [having stopped in the middle of the verse], gave it back to the attendant and sat down [to teach]; and the eyes of all those in the synagogue were [attentively] fixed on Him.
AMP.21: He began speaking to them: "Today this Scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing and in your presence."
AMP.22: And [as He continued on] they all were speaking well of Him, and were in awe and were wondering about the words of grace which were coming from His lips; and they were saying, "Is this not Joseph's son?"
AMP.23: So He said to them, "You will no doubt quote this proverb to Me, 'Physician, heal Yourself! Whatever [miracles] that we heard were done [by You] in Capernaum, do here in Your hometown as well.'"
AMP.24: Then He said, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, no prophet is welcome in his hometown.
AMP.25: But in truth I say to you, there were many widows in Israel in the days of Elijah, when the sky was closed up for three years and six months, when a great famine came over all the land;
AMP.26: and yet Elijah was not sent [by the Lord] to a single one of them, but only to Zarephath in the land of Sidon, to a woman who was a widow.
AMP.27: And there were many lepers in Israel in the time of Elisha the prophet; and not one of them was cleansed [by being healed] except Naaman the Syrian."
AMP.28: As they heard these things [about God's grace to these two Gentiles], the people in the synagogue were filled with a great rage;
AMP.29: and they got up and drove Him out of the city, and led Him to the crest of the hill on which their city had been built, in order to hurl Him down the cliff.
AMP.30: But passing [miraculously] through the crowd, He went on His way.
AMP.31: Then He came down [from the hills of Nazareth] to Capernaum, a city of Galilee [on the shore of the sea], and He was teaching them on the Sabbath;
AMP.32: and they were surprised [almost overwhelmed] at His teaching, because His message was [given] with authority and power and great ability.
AMP.33: There was a man in the synagogue who was possessed by the spirit of an unclean demon; and he cried out with a loud and terrible voice,
AMP.34: "Let us alone! What business do we have [in common] with each other, Jesus of Nazareth? Have You come to destroy us? I know who You are—the Holy One of God!"
AMP.35: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, "Be silent (muzzled, gagged) and come out of him!" And when the demon had thrown the man down among them, he came out of him without injuring him in any way.
AMP.36: They were all astonished and in awe, and began saying to one another, "What is this message? For with authority and power He commands the unclean spirits and they come out!"
AMP.37: And the news about Him spread into every place in the surrounding district (Galilee).
AMP.38: Then Jesus got up and left the synagogue and went to Simon's (Peter's) house. Now Simon's mother-in-law was suffering from a high fever, and they asked Him to help her.
AMP.39: Standing over her, He rebuked the fever, and it left her; and immediately she got up and began serving them [as her guests].
AMP.40: While the sun was setting [marking the end of the Sabbath day], all those who had any who were sick with various diseases brought them to Jesus; and laying His hands on each one of them, He was healing them [exhibiting His authority as Messiah].
AMP.41: Demons also were coming out of many people, shouting, "You are the Son of God!" But He rebuked them and would not allow them to speak, because they knew that He was the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed).
AMP.42: When daybreak came, Jesus left [Simon Peter's house] and went to a secluded place; and the crowds were searching for Him, and [they] came to Him and tried to keep Him from leaving them.
AMP.43: But He said, "I must preach [the good news of] the kingdom of God to the other cities also, because I was sent for this purpose."
AMP.44: So He continued preaching in the synagogues of Judea [the country of the Jews, including Galilee].

MSG.1: Now Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, left the Jordan and was led by the Spirit into the wild.
MSG.2: For forty wilderness days and nights he was tested by the Devil. He ate nothing during those days, and when the time was up he was hungry.
MSG.3: The Devil, playing on his hunger, gave the first test: "Since you're God's Son, command this stone to turn into a loaf of bread."
MSG.4: Jesus answered by quoting Deuteronomy: "It takes more than bread to really live."
MSG.5: For the second test he led him up and spread out all the kingdoms of the earth on display at once.
MSG.6: Then the Devil said, "They're yours in all their splendor to serve your pleasure. I'm in charge of them all and can turn them over to whomever I wish.
MSG.7: Worship me and they're yours, the whole works."
MSG.8: Jesus refused, again backing his refusal with Deuteronomy: "Worship the Lord your God and only the Lord your God. Serve him with absolute single-heartedness."
MSG.9: For the third test the Devil took him to Jerusalem and put him on top of the Temple. He said, "If you are God's Son, jump.
MSG.10: It's written, isn't it, that 'he has placed you in the care of angels to protect you;
MSG.11: they will catch you; you won't so much as stub your toe on a stone'?"
MSG.12: "Yes," said Jesus, "and it's also written, 'Don't you dare tempt the Lord your God.'"
MSG.13: That completed the testing. The Devil retreated temporarily, lying in wait for another opportunity.
MSG.14: Jesus returned to Galilee powerful in the Spirit. News that he was back spread through the countryside.
MSG.15: He taught in their meeting places to everyone's acclaim and pleasure.
MSG.16: He came to Nazareth where he had been reared. As he always did on the Sabbath, he went to the meeting place. When he stood up to read,
MSG.17: he was handed the scroll of the prophet Isaiah. Unrolling the scroll, he found the place where it was written,
MSG.18: God's Spirit is on me; he's chosen me to preach the Message of good news to the poor, Sent me to announce pardon to prisoners and recovery of sight to the blind, To set the burdened and battered free,
MSG.19: to announce, "This is God's year to act!"
MSG.20: He rolled up the scroll, handed it back to the assistant, and sat down. Every eye in the place was on him, intent.
MSG.21: Then he started in, "You've just heard Scripture make history. It came true just now in this place."
MSG.22: All who were there, watching and listening, were surprised at how well he spoke. But they also said, "Isn't this Joseph's son, the one we've known since he was a youngster?"
MSG.23: He answered, "I suppose you're going to quote the proverb, 'Doctor, go heal yourself. Do here in your hometown what we heard you did in Capernaum.'
MSG.24: Well, let me tell you something: No prophet is ever welcomed in his hometown.
MSG.25: Isn't it a fact that there were many widows in Israel at the time of Elijah during that three and a half years of drought when famine devastated the land,
MSG.26: but the only widow to whom Elijah was sent was in Sarepta in Sidon?
MSG.27: And there were many lepers in Israel at the time of the prophet Elisha but the only one cleansed was Naaman the Syrian."
MSG.28: That set everyone in the meeting place seething with anger.
MSG.29: They threw him out, banishing him from the village, then took him to a mountain cliff at the edge of the village to throw him to his doom,
MSG.30: but he gave them the slip and was on his way.
MSG.31: He went down to Capernaum, a village in Galilee. He was teaching the people on the Sabbath.
MSG.32: They were surprised and impressed—his teaching was so forthright, so confident, so authoritative, not the quibbling and quoting they were used to.
MSG.33: In the meeting place that day there was a man demonically disturbed. He screamed,
MSG.34: "Ho! What business do you have here with us, Jesus? Nazarene! I know what you're up to. You're the Holy One of God and you've come to destroy us!"
MSG.35: Jesus shut him up: "Quiet! Get out of him!" The demonic spirit threw the man down in front of them all and left. The demon didn't hurt him.
MSG.36: That set everyone back on their heels, whispering and wondering, "What's going on here? Someone whose words make things happen? Someone who orders demonic spirits to get out and they go?"
MSG.37: Jesus was the talk of the town.
MSG.38: He left the meeting place and went to Simon's house. Simon's mother-in-law was running a high fever and they asked him to do something for her.
MSG.39: He stood over her, told the fever to leave—and it left. Before they knew it, she was up getting dinner for them.
MSG.40: When the sun went down, everyone who had anyone sick with some ailment or other brought them to him. One by one he placed his hands on them and healed them.
MSG.41: Demons left in droves, screaming, "Son of God! You're the Son of God!" But he shut them up, refusing to let them speak because they knew too much, knew him to be the Messiah.
MSG.42: He left the next day for open country. But the crowds went looking and, when they found him, clung to him so he couldn't go on.
MSG.43: He told them, "Don't you realize that there are yet other villages where I have to tell the Message of God's kingdom, that this is the work God sent me to do?"
MSG.44: Meanwhile he continued preaching in the meeting places of Galilee.

NIV.1:
The Temptation of Jesus
Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan and was led by the Spirit in the desert,
NIV.2: where for forty days he was tempted by the devil. He ate nothing during those days, and at the end of them he was hungry.
NIV.3: The devil said to him, “If you are the Son of God, tell this stone to become bread.”
NIV.4: Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man does not live on bread alone.’”
NIV.5: The devil led him up to a high place and showed him in an instant all the kingdoms of the world.
NIV.6: And he said to him, “I will give you all their authority and splendor, for it has been given to me, and I can give it to anyone I want to.
NIV.7: So if you worship me, it will all be yours.”
NIV.8: Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve him only.’”
NIV.9: The devil led him to Jerusalem and had him stand on the highest point of the temple. “If you are the Son of God,” he said, “throw yourself down from here.
NIV.10:
For it is written:
“‘He will command his angels concerning you
to guard you carefully;
NIV.11:
they will lift you up in their hands,
so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.’”
NIV.12: Jesus answered, “It says: ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’”
NIV.13: When the devil had finished all this tempting, he left him until an opportune time.
NIV.14:
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
Jesus returned to Galilee in the power of the Spirit, and news about him spread through the whole countryside.
NIV.15: He taught in their synagogues, and everyone praised him.
NIV.16: He went to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and on the Sabbath day he went into the synagogue, as was his custom. And he stood up to read.
NIV.17: The scroll of the prophet Isaiah was handed to him. Unrolling it, he found the place where it is written:
NIV.18:
“The Spirit of the Lord is on me,
because he has anointed me
to preach good news to the poor.
He has sent me to proclaim freedom for the prisoners
and recovery of sight for the blind,
to release the oppressed,
NIV.19: to proclaim the year of the Lord’s favor.”
NIV.20: Then he rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the attendant and sat down. The eyes of everyone in the synagogue were fastened on him,
NIV.21: and he began by saying to them, “Today this scripture is fulfilled in your hearing.”
NIV.22: All spoke well of him and were amazed at the gracious words that came from his lips. “Isn’t this Joseph’s son?” they asked.
NIV.23: Jesus said to them, “Surely you will quote this proverb to me: ‘Physician, heal yourself! Do here in your hometown what we have heard that you did in Capernaum.’”
NIV.24: “I tell you the truth,” he continued, “no prophet is accepted in his hometown.
NIV.25: I assure you that there were many widows in Israel in Elijah’s time, when the sky was shut for three and a half years and there was a severe famine throughout the land.
NIV.26: Yet Elijah was not sent to any of them, but to a widow in Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
NIV.27: And there were many in Israel with leprosy in the time of Elisha the prophet, yet not one of them was cleansed—only Naaman the Syrian.”
NIV.28: All the people in the synagogue were furious when they heard this.
NIV.29: They got up, drove him out of the town, and took him to the brow of the hill on which the town was built, in order to throw him down the cliff.
NIV.30: But he walked right through the crowd and went on his way.
NIV.31:
Jesus Drives Out an Evil Spirit
Then he went down to Capernaum, a town in Galilee, and on the Sabbath began to teach the people.
NIV.32: They were amazed at his teaching, because his message had authority.
NIV.33: In the synagogue there was a man possessed by a demon, an evil spirit. He cried out at the top of his voice,
NIV.34: “Ha! What do you want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are—the Holy One of God!”
NIV.35: “Be quiet!” Jesus said sternly. “Come out of him!” Then the demon threw the man down before them all and came out without injuring him.
NIV.36: All the people were amazed and said to each other, “What is this teaching? With authority and power he gives orders to evil spirits and they come out!”
NIV.37: And the news about him spread throughout the surrounding area.
NIV.38:
Jesus Heals Many
Jesus left the synagogue and went to the home of Simon. Now Simon’s mother-in-law was suffering from a high fever, and they asked Jesus to help her.
NIV.39: So he bent over her and rebuked the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and began to wait on them.
NIV.40: When the sun was setting, the people brought to Jesus all who had various kinds of sickness, and laying his hands on each one, he healed them.
NIV.41: Moreover, demons came out of many people, shouting, “You are the Son of God!” But he rebuked them and would not allow them to speak, because they knew he was the Christ.
NIV.42: At daybreak Jesus went out to a solitary place. The people were looking for him and when they came to where he was, they tried to keep him from leaving them.
NIV.43: But he said, “I must preach the good news of the kingdom of God to the other towns also, because that is why I was sent.”
NIV.44: And he kept on preaching in the synagogues of Judea.

NET.19: to proclaim the year of the Lord’s favor.”
NET.20: Then he rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the attendant, and sat down. The eyes of everyone in the synagogue were fixed on him.
NET.21: Then he began to tell them, “Today this scripture has been fulfilled even as you heard it being read.”
NET.22: All were speaking well of him, and were amazed at the gracious words coming out of his mouth. They said, “Isn’t this Joseph’s son?”
NET.23: Jesus said to them, “No doubt you will quote to me the proverb, ‘Physician, heal yourself!’ and say, ‘What we have heard that you did in Capernaum, do here in your hometown too.’”
NET.24: And he added, “I tell you the truth, no prophet is acceptable in his hometown.
NET.25: But in truth I tell you, there were many widows in Israel in Elijah’s days, when the sky was shut up three and a half years, and there was a great famine over all the land.
NET.26: Yet Elijah was sent to none of them, but only to a woman who was a widow at Zarephath in Sidon.
NET.27: And there were many lepers in Israel in the time of the prophet Elisha, yet none of them was cleansed except Naaman the Syrian.”
NET.28: When they heard this, all the people in the synagogue were filled with rage.
NET.29: They got up, forced him out of the town, and brought him to the brow of the hill on which their town was built, so that they could throw him down the cliff.
NET.30: But he passed through the crowd and went on his way.
NET.31: So he went down to Capernaum, a town in Galilee, and on the Sabbath he began to teach the people.
NET.32: They were amazed at his teaching, because he spoke with authority.
NET.33: Now in the synagogue there was a man who had the spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried out with a loud voice,
NET.34: “Ha! Leave us alone, Jesus the Nazarene! Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are — the Holy One of God.”
NET.35: But Jesus rebuked him: “Silence! Come out of him!” Then, after the demon threw the man down in their midst, he came out of him without hurting him.
NET.36: They were all amazed and began to say to one another, “What’s happening here? For with authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and they come out!”
NET.37: So the news about him spread into all areas of the region.
NET.38: After Jesus left the synagogue, he entered Simon’s house. Now Simon’s mother-in-law was suffering from a high fever, and they asked Jesus to help her.
NET.39: So he stood over her, commanded the fever, and it left her. Immediately she got up and began to serve them.
NET.40: As the sun was setting, all those who had any relatives sick with various diseases brought them to Jesus. He placed his hands on every one of them and healed them.
NET.41: Demons also came out of many, crying out, “You are the Son of God!” But he rebuked them, and would not allow them to speak, because they knew that he was the Christ.
NET.42: The next morning Jesus departed and went to a deserted place. Yet the crowds were seeking him, and they came to him and tried to keep him from leaving them.
NET.43: But Jesus said to them, “I must proclaim the good news of the kingdom of God to the other towns too, for that is what I was sent to do.”
NET.44: So he continued to preach in the synagogues of Judea.
